title: CDS vs NDA: Which Defence Entry is Right for You?
author: DefenceRoger Editorial
data: A comparison between entering the Armed Forces post-12th via NDA vs post-graduation via CDS. NDA offers 3 years of academic training at Khadakwasla followed by 1 year at service academies (IMA/INA/AFA), granting a solid foundation at an early age. CDS offers direct 1 to 1.5 years of intensive training at service academies. NDA is ideal for early decision-makers, while CDS caters to graduates seeking a direct, shorter path to commission. Both lead to the prestigious rank of Lieutenant or equivalent.
